objective: Are there any $2$-full $n$ such that $n+1$ is $3$-full? That is, if $p\mid n$ then $p^2\mid n$ and if $p\mid n+1$ then $p^3\mid n+1$.
completion_criteria: Official database status is verifiable; refine the exact certificate contract before any candidate claim.
non_results: Finite computation outside its stated range is not a proof of a universal claim.
A candidate is not a solution until independent and human review are recorded.
Restating a known reduction, checking examples, or giving an unproved key lemma is not terminal success.

baseline_review.requirement: Repeat when the authoritative status changes materially or the baseline is explicitly invalidated.

synthesis_summary: The official and original statements agree on the target orientation: n is 2-full and n+1 is 3-full. The maintained source remains open. A dense oracle and sparse generator agreed on exactly 307 3-full values through 10^6, with identical canonical SHA-256 hashes and no duplicates. The sparse search then examined 1,645 3-full values through 10^8 and found no target. No universal claim or candidate solution is made.

established_facts.claim: The target orientation is n 2-full followed by n+1 3-full; n=8 is reverse-oriented.
The two independent encodings agree on all 307 3-full integers through 10^6 and find no target.
Only finitely many target pairs exist if abc is true.
established_facts.evidence: Official discussion's radical argument with epsilon<1/5

strategies.title: Mandatory source/status baseline followed by a bounded counterexample search that enumerates 3-full m=n+1, with a separate dense smallest-prime-factor oracle and reverse-orientation controls.
strategies.mechanism: Generate every 3-full m in the bounded domain exactly once from increasing prime supports and exponents at least three, factor m-1 exactly, and independently compare the generated set with a dense sieve oracle.
strategies.hypothesis: The sparse generator is complete and duplicate-free on a nontrivial audited overlap, and either finds a target witness with n+1<=100000000 or certifies an empty hit list only on that bounded domain.
strategies.discriminating_test: Compare the complete generated 3-full set against a dense smallest-prime-factor scan through 10^6, exercise reversed examples, then enumerate all 3-full m through 10^8.
strategies.blocker: No witness is known, and no unconditional theorem reduces universal nonexistence to a finite certified computation. General consecutive-powerful classifications do not enforce the asymmetric 3-full successor condition.
strategies.reopen_condition: Resume undifferentiated bound extension only with a recognized target bound, an explicit request, a completeness-certificate objective, or a new structural pruning result.

strategies.mechanism: Decompose p^e-1 into cyclotomic factors, exploit their controlled gcds, and test whether the powerfulness requirement forces forbidden exponent-one prime divisors.
strategies.hypothesis: At least one exponent or residue class in the single-prime n+1 branch admits a provable local obstruction or a sharply reduced finite set.
strategies.discriminating_test: Compare a symbolic residue/gcd filter with the complete single-prime cases through 10^6 and require exact out-of-sample agreement.
strategies.rationale: A structural pruning lemma would carry more information than another arbitrary global cutoff.
strategies.title: Decompose p^e-1 into cyclotomic factors, exploit their controlled gcds, and test whether the powerfulness requirement forces forbidden exponent-one prime divisors.

strategies.mechanism: Recover or independently rebuild the A060355 enumeration and verify both its coverage and every listed factorization without using the present sparse target code.
strategies.hypothesis: The reported consecutive-powerful table below 10^22 can be converted into a reproducible exhaustive certificate.
strategies.discriminating_test: Locate executable provenance or reproduce a smaller published prefix with a complete independent generator before attempting the full range.
strategies.rationale: This could turn an unverified superset report into a rigorous bounded target exclusion.
strategies.title: Recover or independently rebuild the A060355 enumeration and verify both its coverage and every listed factorization without using the present sparse target code.

open_leads.description: Analyze n+1=p^e using the factorization of p^e-1.
open_leads.rationale: Cyclotomic factor coprimality may yield a genuine obstruction or materially prune a witness branch.
open_leads.next_experiment: Derive gcd conditions, implement a bounded certified shell, and compare with all single-prime-support cases through 10^6.

open_leads.rationale: A certificate-bearing complete powerful-pair list could be filtered into a rigorous problem-366 exclusion.
open_leads.next_experiment: Audit OEIS history and Donovan Johnson table provenance for code and completeness arguments.

open_leads.description: Formalize a future explicit witness in Lean.
open_leads.rationale: The existing reverse example shows numeral instances of Nat.Full can be discharged by normalization.
open_leads.next_experiment: After a witness exists, instantiate the existential and compile with no sorry or admit.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use (8,9), (12167,12168), or generic Pell-generated powerful pairs as target evidence without checking orientation and exponent three.
ruled_out.scope: Known reverse examples and unfiltered consecutive-powerful families
ruled_out.reason: They prove 3-full then 2-full, or only 2-full then 2-full.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Produce a family member whose first number independently passes 2-full and whose successor passes 3-full.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Routine extension of the same arbitrary search cutoff as a contribution.
ruled_out.scope: Larger bounds without a recognized benchmark, structural theorem, or certificate-bearing reproduction
ruled_out.reason: A finite null search cannot settle universal nonexistence and would not improve a verified best-known result.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A predeclared recognized bound, explicit expert request, or new structural pruning theorem.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat OEIS A060355 alone as a certified target exclusion through 10^22.
ruled_out.scope: The current b-file without retained exhaustive-search code
ruled_out.reason: It is a superset table and lacks an independently checked completeness certificate for the reported range.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Recover or reproduce the enumeration with a complete generator and independent certificate.

next_session.objective: Test the single-prime-support successor branch for structural information.
next_session.first_action: Write the exact factor/gcd decomposition of p^e-1 for n+1=p^e, then enumerate e>=3 and p^e<=10^6 as a regression fixture against tools/erdos366_discriminator.py.
next_session.stop_condition: Stop immediately on a verified witness; otherwise stop after the declared shell and retain the route only if it proves a local obstruction or material pruning rule.
